nodes.name: bot.py
nodes.summary: Main bot entry point — registers all Telegram command, callback, and message handlers, manages the bot lifecycle via post_init/post_shutdown, and routes messages between Telegram forum topics and tmux windows running Claude Code sessions. Also handles voice transcription, photo forwarding, screenshot capture, and interactive UI navigation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses python-telegram-bot v20+ Application/Handler pattern with AIORateLimiter. Defines ~20 async handler functions. Hot path (text_handler) must never abort after tmux injection succeeds.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Loads configuration from environment variables with .env file support (local .env > CCBOT_DIR/.env). Exposes a singleton Config instance and scrubs sensitive env vars to prevent credential leaks to child processes.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports dotenv.load_dotenv for .env loading. Scrub loop removes TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/ALLOWED_USERS/OPENAI_API_KEY from os.environ after capture.

nodes.name: callback_data.py
nodes.summary: Defines all CB_* prefix constants for Telegram inline keyboard callback data routing. Each constant encodes a distinct action: history pagination, directory browser navigation, window picker binding, screenshot refresh, interactive UI navigation, and session picking.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure constant definitions with no imports beyond stdlib. Callback payloads must stay under Telegram's 64-byte limit.

nodes.name: directory_browser.py
nodes.summary: Builds inline keyboard UIs for three flows: the directory browser (navigate filesystem to create a new session), the window picker (bind an existing unbound tmux window), and the session picker (resume an existing Claude session). Includes pagination, state keys, and clear-state helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: State keys (STATE_KEY/BROWSE_PATH_KEY/etc.) shared with bot.py via direct import. UiState machine transitions: idle -> browsing_directory -> selecting_session -> create_window.

nodes.name: monitor_state.py
nodes.summary: Persistent state tracker for the session monitor — stores per-session byte offsets (TrackedSession) to enable incremental JSONL reading across restarts. Wraps load/save in a MonitorState dataclass with dirty-flag optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclasses for TrackedSession/MonitorState. Delegates atomic JSON writes to utils.atomic_write_json. Byte-offset tracking avoids re-reading already-processed messages.

nodes.name: screenshot.py
nodes.summary: Renders captured tmux pane text (with ANSI color codes) into a dark-background PNG image using Pillow. Features a three-tier font fallback chain (JetBrains Mono, Noto Sans Mono CJK SC, Symbola) for broad character coverage. ANSI parsing supports 16/256/RGB colors.
nodes.languageNotes: CPU-bound rendering offloaded to thread pool via asyncio.to_thread. Font tiers selected per-codepoint. ANSI parser handles SGR codes (0-107) with extended 256-color and RGB support.

nodes.name: session_monitor.py
nodes.summary: Background async polling service that watches Claude Code JSONL session files for new messages. Scans projects directories, tracks byte offsets via MonitorState, detects session_map changes, and emits NewMessage objects through a callback. Handles file truncation, corrupted offsets, and partial writes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses aiofiles for non-blocking file I/O. Maintains in-memory mtime cache to skip unchanged files. Pending tool_use state carried across poll cycles for tool_use/tool_result pairing.

nodes.name: transcribe.py
nodes.summary: Voice-to-text transcription via the OpenAI audio/transcriptions API using gpt-4o-transcribe model. Uses httpx directly (no OpenAI SDK) with a lazily-initialized async client singleton.
nodes.languageNotes: Single async function wrapping httpx POST to OpenAI. Client is lazily initialized singleton. close_client() called during bot shutdown.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Shared utility functions: ccbot_dir() resolves the config directory from CCBOT_DIR env var, atomic_write_json() performs crash-safe JSON writes via temp-file-then-rename, and read_cwd_from_jsonl() extracts the cwd from the first entry of a JSONL file.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Python stdlib (json, os, tempfile, pathlib). atomic_write_json uses os.replace for atomic cross-filesystem renames.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Application configuration singleton — loads TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, ALLOWED_USERS, TMUX_SESSION_NAME, CLAUDE_COMMAND, monitoring intervals, OpenAI keys, and display preferences from environment variables or .env files.
nodes.languageNotes: Instantiated at module level as `config`. is_user_allowed() is the only method beyond __init__.

nodes.name: TrackedSession
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single tracked Claude Code session with session_id, file_path, and last_byte_offset for incremental JSONL reading. Provides to_dict/from_dict serialization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @dataclass decorator with field defaults. to_dict() delegates to dataclasses.asdict.

nodes.name: MonitorState
nodes.summary: Persistent state container for the session monitor — manages a dict of TrackedSession objects keyed by session_id, with load/save/save_if_dirty lifecycle and dirty-flag optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Dirty flag avoids unnecessary writes. Atomic save via utils.atomic_write_json. load() handles missing/empty files gracefully.

nodes.name: TextStyle
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing ANSI text styling with foreground color and optional background color. Defaults to light gray on dark gray.

nodes.name: StyledSegment
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a text segment with its TextStyle and font_tier index for multi-font rendering.

nodes.name: SessionInfo
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding session_id and file_path for a Claude Code session discovered during project scanning.

nodes.name: NewMessage
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a new message detected by the monitor, with fields for session_id, text, completion status, content_type, tool_use_id, role, tool_name, and optional image data from tool_result images.

nodes.name: SessionMonitor
nodes.summary: Primary monitoring service — runs an async polling loop that scans Claude Code projects, reads new JSONL entries via byte-offset tracking, parses them through TranscriptParser, and emits NewMessage objects. Detects session_map changes and cleans up stale sessions on startup.
nodes.languageNotes: Core loop in _monitor_loop(). Uses aiofiles for non-blocking reads. Maintains per-session pending tool state across cycles. mtime cache for quick no-change detection.

nodes.name: is_user_allowed
nodes.summary: Checks if a Telegram user ID is in the configured allowed_users set.

nodes.name: start_command
nodes.summary: /start handler — shows welcome message after authorizing the user.

nodes.name: history_command
nodes.summary: /history handler — shows paginated message history for the bound session.

nodes.name: screenshot_command
nodes.summary: /screenshot handler — captures the tmux pane content as a PNG image with an inline keyboard for terminal control keys.

nodes.name: unbind_command
nodes.summary: /unbind handler — unbinds the current topic from its tmux window without killing the Claude session.

nodes.name: esc_command
nodes.summary: /esc handler — sends the Escape control character to the tmux pane to interrupt Claude.

nodes.name: usage_command
nodes.summary: /usage handler — sends /usage to Claude Code, captures the terminal output, and parses structured usage info or falls back to raw pane text.

nodes.name: topic_closed_handler
nodes.summary: Handles forum topic closure — kills the associated tmux window and cleans up all per-topic state.

nodes.name: topic_edited_handler
nodes.summary: Handles forum topic rename — syncs the new topic name to the tmux window name and internal SessionManager state.

nodes.name: forward_command_handler
nodes.summary: Forwards any unknown /command (e.g. /clear, /compact, /cost) to Claude Code as a slash command via tmux, enabling remote Claude interaction.

nodes.name: unsupported_content_handler
nodes.summary: Replies to unsupported media types (stickers, video, etc.) with a warning message listing supported types.

nodes.name: photo_handler
nodes.summary: Downloads incoming photos to ~/.ccbot/images/ and forwards the file path to Claude Code via tmux, optionally with a text caption.

nodes.name: voice_handler
nodes.summary: Downloads voice messages, transcribes them via OpenAI API, and forwards the transcribed text to Claude Code.

nodes.name: text_handler
nodes.summary: Primary text message handler — routes user text to a bound tmux window, or shows the window picker/directory browser for unbound topics. Handles state machine transitions, bash command capture, and interactive UI pre-checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Hot path: must never abort after tmux injection. Manages three UI state machines (window-picker, directory-browser, session-picker). Triggers background bash output capture for !-prefixed commands.

nodes.name: callback_handler
nodes.summary: Central callback query dispatcher — routes all inline keyboard interactions (directory browser, window picker, session picker, screenshot keys, history pagination, and interactive UI navigation) based on CB_* prefix constants.
nodes.languageNotes: Single dispatcher function matching ~20 callback prefixes. Validates topic binding freshness to prevent stale interactions. Handles both old and new history pagination formats.

nodes.name: handle_new_message
nodes.summary: Callback invoked by SessionMonitor for each new Claude message — routes to the correct user topic, handles interactive UI tools specially, filters by CCBOT_SHOW_TOOL_CALLS, and enqueues content messages for sequential delivery.
nodes.languageNotes: Checks interactive tools first before normal content path. Builds response parts via build_response_parts, then enqueues via enqueue_content_message. Updates user read offsets after delivery.

nodes.name: post_init
nodes.summary: Bot startup lifecycle hook — sets bot commands, resolves stale window IDs, pre-fills the rate limiter bucket, starts the SessionMonitor and the status polling loop.

nodes.name: post_shutdown
nodes.summary: Bot shutdown lifecycle hook — cancels status polling, shuts down message queue workers, stops the session monitor, and closes the transcribe HTTP client.

nodes.name: create_bot
nodes.summary: Application factory — builds a Telegram Application with AIORateLimiter, post_init/post_shutdown hooks, and all command/callback/message handlers registered.

nodes.name: clear_browse_state
nodes.summary: Clears all directory browsing state keys from user_data.

nodes.name: clear_window_picker_state
nodes.summary: Clears window picker state keys from user_data.

nodes.name: clear_session_picker_state
nodes.summary: Clears session picker state keys from user_data.

nodes.name: build_window_picker
nodes.summary: Builds an inline keyboard UI listing unbound tmux windows for quick binding, with New Session and Cancel buttons.

nodes.name: build_directory_browser
nodes.summary: Builds a paginated inline keyboard directory browser UI with folder navigation, Select, Cancel, and page controls.

nodes.name: build_session_picker
nodes.summary: Builds an inline keyboard UI for selecting an existing Claude session to resume, with relative time and message count info.

nodes.name: text_to_image
nodes.summary: Renders monospace terminal text (with optional ANSI color codes) onto a dark-background PNG image using a three-tier font fallback chain. Offloads CPU-bound Pillow rendering to a thread pool.

nodes.name: transcribe_voice
nodes.summary: Transcribes OGG voice data to text via the OpenAI audio/transcriptions API using the gpt-4o-transcribe model.

nodes.name: close_client
nodes.summary: Closes the lazily-initialized httpx AsyncClient singleton. Called during bot shutdown.

nodes.name: ccbot_dir
nodes.summary: Returns the configuration directory path from CCBOT_DIR env var, defaulting to ~/.ccbot.

nodes.name: atomic_write_json
nodes.summary: Atomically writes JSON data to a file using a temp-file-then-rename pattern to prevent corruption.

nodes.name: read_cwd_from_jsonl
nodes.summary: Extracts the cwd field from the first JSONL entry that contains one.

nodes.name: Topic-to-Session Routing
nodes.summary: The 1:1 mapping between Telegram forum topics and tmux windows. Each topic is bound to one tmux window running one Claude Code instance, with per-user thread bindings stored in session_manager state.
nodes.languageNotes: Keyed by tmux window ID (@0, @12), not window name. No backward-compat for non-topic mode. Group chat_id required for supergroup message routing.

nodes.name: Session Monitoring via JSONL Polling
nodes.summary: Async polling loop that watches Claude Code's JSONL session files, detects new entries via byte-offset tracking, parses them through TranscriptParser, and emits NewMessage objects for delivery to users.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses aiofiles for non-blocking reads. Handles file truncation (/clear), corrupted offsets, and partial writes. Maintains per-session pending tool_use state across poll cycles.

nodes.name: Per-User Message Queue
nodes.summary: FIFO message queue per Telegram user with message merging (3800-char limit) and tool_use/tool_result pairing. Ensures status messages always appear last in the queue.

nodes.name: Callback Query Routing
nodes.summary: Central callback handler dispatches all inline keyboard interactions by matching CB_* prefix constants. Validates topic binding freshness and supports both old/new callback data formats.
nodes.languageNotes: Callback payloads must stay under Telegram's 64-byte limit. Prefix-based dispatching with topic-mismatch validation.

nodes.name: Directory Browser UI
nodes.summary: Paginated inline keyboard interface for navigating the filesystem to select a working directory for a new Claude Code session. Supports hidden directory toggling and parent directory navigation.

nodes.name: Terminal Interactive UI Handling
nodes.summary: Detects and renders terminal-based interactive UIs (permission prompts, selection menus, model pickers) as Telegram inline keyboards for remote interaction with tk or text-based UIs.

nodes.name: Screenshot Rendering
nodes.summary: Renders captured tmux pane text (with ANSI color codes) into a dark-background PNG image using Pillow with multi-font fallback support.

nodes.name: ANSI Color Parsing
nodes.summary: Parses ANSI escape code SGR sequences (16 colors, 256-color palette, and full RGB) into TextStyle objects for rendering. Handles 0-107 color codes with bold/bright variants.

nodes.name: Voice Message Transcription
nodes.summary: Transcribes OGG voice messages to text via the OpenAI audio/transcriptions API using gpt-4o-transcribe, then forwards the result to Claude Code.

nodes.name: State Persistence
nodes.summary: Crash-safe persistent storage for thread bindings (state.json), session_map (session_map.json), and monitor byte offsets (monitor_state.json). Uses atomic temp-file-rename writes to prevent corruption.
nodes.languageNotes: Three state files under CCBOT_DIR. atomic_write_json ensures crash safety. Monitor state uses dirty-flag optimization.

nodes.name: Telegram API Rate Limiting
nodes.summary: Uses AIORateLimiter with max_retries=5 to handle Telegram's server-side rate limits. Pre-fills the bucket on restart to match Telegram's persisted counter.
nodes.languageNotes: AIORateLimiter from python-telegram-bot. Bucket pre-fill hack sets _level=max_rate on restart.

nodes.name: Terminal Status Polling
nodes.summary: Background polling loop that periodically checks the Claude Code terminal status line and sends/replies to users with status updates. Detects interactive UIs and idle states.

nodes.name: Environment-Based Configuration
nodes.summary: Centralized configuration loaded from environment variables with .env file support, providing bot token, user allowlist, tmux paths, monitoring intervals, and feature flags to all modules.

nodes.name: User Authorization
nodes.summary: Access control via the ALLOWED_USERS environment variable — a comma-separated list of Telegram user IDs. Every handler checks is_user_allowed() before processing.

nodes.name: Background Bash Output Capture
nodes.summary: Background asyncio task that captures the output of !-prefixed bash commands from the tmux pane, sending incremental updates to the user as a streaming message that gets edited in-place.
